---
title: "Research"
pagetitle: "Research | Peleg Kremer"
---

::: {.lead}
We use GIS, remote sensing, and machine learning to study how cities shape exposure to environmental burdens (heat, flooding, air pollution) and access to environmental benefits (parks, cooling, green space). Most fieldwork is in Philadelphia, with comparative work across US and global cities.
:::

## Active projects

::: {.theme-grid}

::: {.theme-card}
::: {.funder-tag}
NSF SCC-IRG · Track 1
:::
### [Integrating Community Dynamics, Environmental Data, and AI to Advance GSI Sustainability](projects/gsi-nsf.qmd)
Co-PI with Smith (PI), Jiao, Wadzuk · 2024 to 2028
:::

::: {.theme-card}
::: {.funder-tag}
NSF DISES
:::
### [Abating Mobility Equity Gaps Induced by Nuisance Flooding](projects/dises-nsf.qmd)
Co-PI with Ermagun and Xiong (PIs), Smith, Wadzuk · 2024 to 2028
:::

::: {.theme-card}
::: {.funder-tag}
William Penn Foundation
:::
### [Beyond Land Precarity: Philadelphia Garden Database and Platform](projects/pgdc.qmd)
PI with Borowiak (Co-PI) · 2025 to 2027
:::

::: {.theme-card}
::: {.funder-tag}
Ongoing
:::
### [STURLA: Urban structure and ecosystem services](projects/ues.qmd)
Methodological and substantive work, including a current single-author global analysis (in review at *Urban Climate*).
:::

:::

## Editorial and service

Associate Editor, *[Sustainable Cities and Society](https://www.sciencedirect.com/journal/sustainable-cities-and-society)* (2025 – present) · Lead Editor, *Sustaining Cities Under Environmental Extremes* special issue (2024 – 2026) · NSF HEGS senior review panel (2024) and ad hoc reviewer (2026). Fuller list on the [About](about.qmd) page.
